# Group Dynamics

The tensions, alliances, and pairing dynamics among the main cast. Character files describe individuals; this file describes the *relationships* — which is where ensemble scenes live or die.

Update when a story shifts a relationship (new tension, resolved conflict, deepened bond).

---

## Core Tensions & Alliances

### [Character A] & [Character B] ([the dynamic's name — e.g., "Tradition vs. Tech"])
- [What the friction or bond is built on. What each one believes that the other doesn't.]
- [How it typically plays out in a scene — playful clash, quiet rivalry, protective alliance.]
- [The asymmetry: what does one hide from the other, envy in the other, or misread about the other?]

### [Character C] & The Group ([their role — e.g., "The Chaos Agent"])
- [What function this character serves in ensemble scenes, and how each other member reacts to it. A role is stronger than a trait: it predicts behavior in scenes you haven't written yet.]

## Other Key Dynamics

- **[A] & [B]:** [One or two sentences on a secondary pairing worth remembering.]
- **[Character]'s role:** [What the group relies on them for — and what they'd never ask them for.]

## Pairing Notes for Scene Writing

[Optional: which two-character combinations are unwritten gold, which are volatile, which have never had a scene alone together. Ensemble casts have O(n²) pairs — track the ones with charge.]
